title: rosa gymnocarpa var. pinetorum
intro: Rosa gymnocarpa var. pinetorum, also known as the pine-rose or pine rose rose, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the rose species that is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This shrub-like plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing pain and fighting off infections. The health benefits of Rosa gymnocarpa var. pinetorum are thought to include reducing fever and relieving digestive issues, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and may be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
